title: Horseback Riding in Greater Austin
subtitle: Trail rides, lesson barns, and equine experiences

popup.title: Greater Austin's Riding Scene
popup.subtitle: 17 public-facing operations within an hour of downtown
popup.body: Greater Austin offers a dense cluster of recreational horseback riding options — from guided ranch trail rides minutes from downtown to structured hunter–jumper lesson barns and wellness-focused equine programs at a luxury resort. Facilities are concentrated along four corridors: southeast toward Bluff Springs and Lockhart, northeast toward Manor, west toward Bee Cave and Dripping Springs, and south toward Buda and Driftwood.

popup.title: Southeast Corridor
popup.subtitle: Bluff Springs, Lockhart, and Del Valle
popup.body: The southeast corridor is the strongest fit for visitors seeking a classic "Texas ranch trail ride" experience within a short drive of downtown. Texas Trail Rides / Lone Star Ranch on Bluff Springs Road offers family-friendly guided trail rides alongside ATV tours, skeet shooting, and corporate events. Maverick Horseback Riding near Lockhart pairs ecology-focused trail rides with BBQ rides on a large Central Texas ranch. Rio Vista Farm, one of Austin's longest-running English barns, has relocated to Dripping Springs but continues to draw riders from this corridor.

stats.label: Key operators
stats.value: Texas Trail Rides, Maverick, Rio Vista Farm

popup.title: Northeast Corridor — Manor
popup.subtitle: Southern Trails, Manor Equestrian, and White Fences
popup.body: To the northeast, Manor and the Decker Lake area host both lesson barns and trail-ride operators catering to families and new riders. Southern Trails offers highly rated guided ranch rides on a 100–120-acre property just 15 minutes from downtown, with kids' "Little Dude" rides and scenic sunset options. Manor Equestrian Center provides family-friendly lessons, boarding, and summer camps on Cameron Road. White Fences Equestrian Center, also in Manor, is a boutique dressage and hunter/jumper barn for committed recreational and show riders.

stats.label: Key operators
stats.value: Southern Trails, Manor Equestrian, White Fences

popup.title: Western Corridor — Bee Cave & Dripping Springs
popup.subtitle: Hill Country barns with English programs and premium boarding
popup.body: The western corridor features Hill Country barns that emphasize English riding, show programs, and premium boarding. Bee Cave Riding Center on Hamilton Pool Road offers hunter/jumper, dressage, and western instruction for all levels. Rio Vista Farm in Dripping Springs — one of Austin's leading English barns — accepts riders without their own horses. Coraggio Equestrian brings 100+ acres of shaded Hill Country trails and individualized training. Lone Star Riding Club in Driftwood is a United States Pony Club Riding Center with multiple area locations and English and Western lessons for children and adults.

stats.label: Key operators
stats.value: Bee Cave RC, Rio Vista, Coraggio, Lone Star RC

popup.title: North, South & Specialty
popup.subtitle: Tri-Star, Switch Willo, Miraval, Scissortail, Monarch, and Austin Carriage
popup.body: North Austin hosts Tri-Star Farm (Spicewood Springs Road), offering lessons, boarding, and weekly trail rides, and Switch Willo Stables, a long-established hunter/jumper show barn running state-certified youth camps since the 1960s. Overlooking Lake Travis, Miraval Austin Resort integrates rhythmic riding and equine-assisted mindfulness into an all-inclusive wellness retreat. South Austin brings Scissortail Hill Equestrian's eventing and hunter/jumper academy and Monarch Stables' 56-acre facility in Buda. Downtown visitors can experience Austin Carriage's horse-drawn tours along Red River Street.

stats.label: Key operators
stats.value: Tri-Star, Switch Willo, Miraval, Scissortail, Monarch, Austin Carriage

popup.title: Find your ride
popup.subtitle: Read the full research report
popup.body: All 17 operators are profiled in depth in the full report — with guidance on choosing between one-time trail rides and recurring lessons, English vs. Western focus, distance from central Austin, and age and weight restrictions to check before booking.
